Sirentide 🌊

Living, narratable diagrams — baked to static SVG, no runtime JS.

Sixteen diagram types · pure-Java bake · inert svg/path/rect/line output · every label a real glyph path · real LaTeX in any label.
Every image below is live renderer output, baked by Sirentide from the DSL beside it.

Piepie legend

Proportional wedges, on-slice contrast labels, an optional left color key.

pie legend
"Reviews" : 40
"Builds" : 25
"Docs" : 20
"Design" : 15

Bars · Lines · Scatterxychart line

One type, three render modes, multi-series with a legend — and a missing value is an honest gap, never a fake bridge.

xychart line legend
series: Revenue, Cost
"Mon" : 5 3
"Tue" : 8 6
"Wed" : 6
"Thu" : 9 4
"Fri" : 12 7

Timelinetimeline

Events placed proportionally in time; ISO dates render as dates.

timeline
"Founded" : 2019-06-01
"Series A" : 2021-03-15
"Launch" : 2024-11-08

Ganttgantt

Min-normalized shared time axis; per-task colors.

gantt
"Design" : 0-3
"Build" : 3-8 #22c55e
"Test" : 7-11
"Ship" : 11-13

Flowchartflowchart

Layered graph, TD or LR — diamond decisions, per-hop edge labels, and a cycle-tolerant layout: the retry loop rides a visible side lane. Node labels auto-contrast with any fill you pick.

flowchart
Start[Commit] --> Test{Tests green?}
Test -->|yes| Ship[Ship it] #22c55e
Test -->|no| Fix[Fix] #ef4444
Fix -->|retry| Test

Flowchart — node shapesflowchart

The mermaid node vocabulary, keyed by delimiter: [rect], (rounded), ([stadium]), ((circle)), {{hexagon}}, [(database)], [[subroutine]], and the {diamond} decision — each baked to pure path/line geometry, labels auto-fitted inside.

flowchart TD
A[Process] --> B(Rounded)
B --> C([Stadium])
C --> D((Go))
D --> E{{Prepare}}
E --> F[(Store)]
F --> G[[Validate]]
G --> H{OK?}

Flowchart — edge typesflowchart

The mermaid edge vocabulary, keyed by operator: --> solid arrow, --- open link (no head), -.-> dotted arrow, -.- dotted open, ==> thick arrow, === thick open — each still carries an optional |label|. Dotted lines are baked as deterministic short segments (the output has no stroke-dasharray); thick lines draw a heavier stroke; open links drop the arrowhead.

flowchart TD
A[Start] --- B[Link]
B -.->|retry| C[Retry]
C -.- D[Idle]
D ==>|ship| E[Ship]
E === F[Done]

Sequencesequence

Actors, lifelines, calls (filled heads), replies (open-V), self-message hooks, and activation bars that grow while an actor is busy.

sequence
Client ->> Gateway : GET /token
Gateway ->> Auth : validate
Auth -->> Gateway : ok
Gateway ->> Gateway : sign JWT
Gateway -->> Client : 200 token

Statestate

Mermaid-style lifecycles: [*] start disc and end bullseye, labeled transitions, cycles inherited from the flowchart engine.

state
[*] --> Idle
Idle --> Running : start
Running --> Idle : stop
Running --> [*]

Quadrantquadrant

A 2×2 positioning matrix: axis-end labels, four quadrant labels (Mermaid numbering — Q1 top-right…Q4 bottom-right), and [x,y] points in the unit square. Soft quadrant tints with contrast-derived labels.

quadrant
x-axis "Low Reach" --> "High Reach"
y-axis "Low Impact" --> "High Impact"
quadrant-1 "Major project"
quadrant-2 "Quick win"
quadrant-3 "Deprioritize"
quadrant-4 "Fill-in"
"Feature A" : [0.3, 0.6]
"Feature B" : [0.75, 0.8]
"Feature C" : [0.5, 0.2]
"Feature D" : [0.85, 0.35]

Comparison matrixmatrix

A categorical verdict matrix: cols: headers over "row" : v1, v2 cells, each filled from a CLOSED palette — pass/fail/partial/na (aliases match→pass, diverge→fail). Rows rectangularize to the header width; an unknown token falls to the neutral fill, so no free-form colour is ever introduced.

matrix
cols: snapshot, bare
"ID1 claim-on-no-signal" : match, match
"PC2 peer-over-flagship" : match, match
"PC1 soft-intent threshold" : partial, diverge
"PC5 boundary-holds-vs-Charles" : match, diverge

Class diagramclassDiagram

UML classes with stacked attribute + method compartments, grid-placed, wired by all five relationship markers — inheritance (hollow triangle), composition (filled diamond), aggregation (hollow diamond), association (arrow), dependency (dashed).

classDiagram
class Animal {
+String name
+int age
+eat() void
+sleep()
}
class Dog {
+bark() void
}
Animal <|-- Dog : inherits
Animal *-- Collar : composition
Animal o-- Owner : aggregation
Dog --> Bone : association
Dog ..> Vet : dependency

ER diagramerDiagram

Entity tables with typed attributes and PK markers, joined by crow-foot cardinality glyphs — exactly-one (bar), zero-or-many (circle + foot), one-or-many (bar + foot).

erDiagram
CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : places
ORDER ||--|{ LINE-ITEM : contains
CUSTOMER }o--o| ADDRESS : has
CUSTOMER {
string name PK
string email
int age
}
ORDER {
int id PK
date created
}

Class diagram — tall mathclassDiagram

A compartment row GROWS to contain a tall multi-row fragment — a matrix, cases, or a stacked fraction — reusing the same math seam the flowchart nodes use. The rows below it shift down, the box grows, and the relationship anchor tracks the taller border. Short / plain labels stay byte-identical (they never grow).

classDiagram
class Matrix {
+grid $\begin{matrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{matrix}$
+int rank
+det() double
}
Matrix --> Scalar : maps

Display mathmathblock

A standalone full-size equation — the whole body is one LaTeX expression, baked centered to real glyph paths and a fraction-bar <rect>. Paste an equation, get an SVG.

mathblock
\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}

Git graphgitGraph

A commit history: dots on a shared time axis, one color-coded lane per branch. branch forks a new lane off the current tip, merge elbows back in with a merge commit, and commit id: "x" labels a dot.

gitGraph
commit
commit id: "init"
branch develop
checkout develop
commit
commit id: "feature"
checkout main
merge develop
commit id: "release"

User journeyjourney

A satisfaction map: tasks in order along the x-axis, each scored 1–5 on the y-axis (higher = happier), connected by a line. section brackets group the steps; each task lists its actors. Points are colored by score — warm red for a low, cool green for a high.

journey
title My working day
section Go to work
Make tea: 5: Me
Commute: 3: Me, Cat
Arrive: 4: Me
section Do work
Code: 5: Me
Meetings: 2: Me, Boss
Lunch: 4: Me, Team

Mindmapmindmap

An indentation-defined hierarchy — the first line is the root, each deeper line a child of the nearest shallower one. Laid out as a left-to-right tree: depth is a column, siblings stack down the y-axis, each parent centered on its children, elbow connectors wiring the branches. Boxes band by depth.

mindmap
  root Mindmaps
    Origins
      Long history
      Popular
    Research
      On effectiveness
    Tools
      Pen and paper
      Mermaid

Sankeysankey

Weighted flows between nodes: each row is source,target,value, and a band's width is proportional to its value. Nodes fall into columns by depth (sources left, sinks right); a node's height is the larger of its in- or out-flow. Bands are a lighter tint of their source's color.

sankey
Coal,Electricity,25
Gas,Electricity,15
Electricity,Homes,20
Electricity,Industry,20
Solar,Homes,10
Solar,Industry,5

Flowchart — nested subgraphssubgraph

Cluster containers group nodes inside a titled frame; nest them for pipelines within pipelines. The frame border, title tab, and canvas grow-to-fit are all baked.

flowchart TD
A[Start] --> B[Work]
subgraph outer [Build Pipeline]
B --> C[Compile]
subgraph inner [Test Suite]
C --> D[Unit]
D --> F[Integration]
end
F --> G[Package]
end
G --> E[Ship]

Flowchart — semantic colour classesclassDef · class

Define a reusable fill with classDef <name> fill:#rrggbb, then assign it with class <id> <name> — the green=allow / red=deny palette the security diagrams need. Same #rrggbb-only hex gate as a per-node colour; a per-node #hex still wins over its class.

flowchart LR
classDef deny fill:#fecaca
classDef ok fill:#bbf7d0
A[Request] --> B{Authorized?}
B -->|yes| C[Serve]
B -->|no| D[Deny]
class C ok
class D deny

Caption / note directive%% caption · %% note

A %% caption: <text> directive (alias %% note:) in the preamble renders a centered, word-wrapped annotation band below any diagram type. It bakes to currentColor glyph paths like every label — inert by construction, no sanitizer change.

%% caption: A merge lands only after both peers approve and no conflicts remain.
flowchart LR
A[Author] --> B[Review]
B --> C[Merge]

Sequence — alt / loop / par framesalt · loop · par

Combined-fragment frames: an alt/else branch, a loop nested inside it, and a par/and across a third actor — each a bordered frame with a label tab and dashed dividers.

sequence
Alice ->> Bob : hello
alt is available
Bob -->> Alice : yes
loop every retry
Alice ->> Bob : ping
end
else is busy
Bob -->> Alice : later
end
par to Bob
Alice ->> Bob : a
and to Carol
Alice ->> Carol : b
end

Sequence — notes & create / destroynote · create · destroy

Annotate a lifeline with a note box — over one actor, spanning over A,B, or to the left of/right of a lifeline — and spin a participant up mid-diagram with create, then end its lifeline with an X via destroy.

sequence
Alice ->> Bob : hello
note right of Bob : Bob validates
note over Alice,Bob : a shared checkpoint
create participant Worker
Bob ->> Worker : spawn job
Worker -->> Bob : started
destroy Worker
Bob -->> Alice : done

Math in any label$…$

The moat: real LaTeX inside a diagram label. A $…$ run in any node/edge/message label bakes to STIX glyph paths (not raw source) via an injected math renderer — here $E=mc^2$ and a real fraction $\frac{v^2}{r}$.

flowchart TD
A[Energy $E=mc^2$] --> B[$\frac{v^2}{r}$]

Multi-row math in labelsmatrix · cases

TALL-fragment box growth: a multi-row construct — a $\begin{matrix}…$, $\begin{cases}…$, a stacked fraction — grows its node box VERTICALLY to fit, keeping the fragment centered on the baseline and fully contained. A single-line label (the Vector and Solve text, the inline $x$) keeps the fixed height, so only the genuinely tall labels grow.

flowchart TD
A[Vector $\begin{matrix} a \\ b \\ c \end{matrix}$] --> B[Scale by $x$]
A --> C[Solve $\begin{cases} x & a \\ y & b \\ z & c \end{cases}$]

One bake, any theme

The same SVG, twice — no media queries, no second render. Page-background text is currentColor (inherits the theme); labels on filled shapes auto-contrast with the fill; author colors (#hex per node, nodecolor= for all) stay legible anywhere.

flowchart nodecolor=#1e293b
A[Author] --> B{Bake}
B -->|svg| C[Docs] #22c55e
light page
dark page

Config block%% theme: dark

A leading %% key: value config block (read before any diagram type) sets title, theme (default·dark·neutral) and direction. theme: dark bakes a self-contained dark background rect + light text, so the SVG reads on a dark page with no media query or second render. Both panes below sit on a dark surface — the default (transparent) needs a light page; theme: dark carries its own.

%% title: Where the time goes
%% theme: dark
pie legend
  "Reviews" : 40
  "Builds" : 30
  "Docs" : 30
default theme · on a dark page
%% theme: dark · self-contained

Play-through framesrenderFrames · seq → N static SVGs

The flow you play: the semantic data-sirentide-seq step-ordering baked into every diagram is now consumed — Sirentide.renderFrames(dsl) returns one static SVG per step, each accenting the active step (thick accent arrow), showing earlier steps done and dimming later ones. No new syntax, no runtime JS, same svg/path/rect/line alphabet — a slideshow a doc flips through. Three consecutive frames below, the active message advancing.

sequence
Client ->> Server : request
Server ->> Server : process
Server -->> Client : response
frame 1 · step 1
frame 2 · step 2
frame 3 · step 3
Clean-room, zero-dependency, Apache-2.0 · sibling of LatteX · gallery of browser-audited renders in examples/gallery
